prevalence.population: EU adult population (~447 million total, focused on those aged 55+)
prevalence.source: European Society of Cardiology (ESC) Heart Failure Association, 2021 ESC Guidelines for the diagnosis and treatment of acute and chronic heart failure
prevalence.trend: increasing due to aging population and improved survival from acute cardiac events

incidence.source: ESC Heart Failure 2022 update and Global Burden of Disease Study (GBD) 2019

burden.source: Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation (IHME) Global Burden of Disease (GBD) 2019, extrapolated to EU

vulnerablePopulations: Patients with comorbidities like diabetes or CKD
Rural populations with limited access to care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: ESC guidelines: Symptoms of heart failure (e.g., dyspnea, fatigue) plus objective evidence of cardiac dysfunction (e.g., echocardiography showing reduced ejection fraction <40% or elevated natriuretic peptides)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: No routine population screening; targeted screening for high-risk groups (e.g., post-MI patients) covers ~60-70% in urban areas
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 40-50% of patients achieve optimal guideline-directed medical therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ESC (European Society of Cardiology) diagnostic standards
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: ESC treatment guidelines and medication protocols
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: EU-specific challenges (aging population, healthcare disparities between Eastern and Western Europe, rising multimorbidity)
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: EU healthcare system strengths for heart failure management: Comprehensive cardiology networks, high access to advanced therapies like SGLT2 inhibitors and device implants, integrated care models in many member states
